column1: # Source: CPIC (https://cpicpgx.org/guidelines/). Positions should be verified against PharmGKB before clinical use.

column6: clinical_summary
CYP2C9 poor/intermediate metabolizers need lower warfarin doses to avoid bleeding risk.
VKORC1 -1639G>A (rs9923231) AA genotype associated with lower dose requirements.
CYP2C19 poor metabolizers have reduced activation of clopidogrel; consider alternative antiplatelet.
CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers convert codeine to morphine too quickly (toxicity risk); poor metabolizers get inadequate pain relief.
SLCO1B1 *5 carriers have increased simvastatin levels and myopathy risk; consider lower dose or alternative statin.
CYP2D6 poor metabolizers have reduced conversion to active endoxifen; consider alternative endocrine therapy.
CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers may have reduced ondansetron efficacy.
DPYD poor metabolizers at high risk of severe/fatal toxicity; dose reduction or alternative required.
TPMT poor metabolizers at risk of life-threatening myelosuppression; start at reduced dose.
CYP2C19 poor metabolizers may need lower sertraline dose; ultra-rapid metabolizers may need higher dose or alternative.
CYP2C19 rapid/ultra-rapid metabolizers may need increased omeprazole dose for H. pylori.
CYP2D6 poor metabolizers have increased metoprolol exposure; consider dose reduction.
CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers at risk of respiratory depression; poor metabolizers get reduced analgesia.
CYP2C9 poor metabolizers have increased NSAID exposure and GI bleeding risk.
CYP2C9 poor metabolizers need reduced phenytoin dose to avoid toxicity.
BCHE deficiency causes prolonged neuromuscular blockade after succinylcholine.
RYR1 pathogenic variants predispose to malignant hyperthermia with volatile anesthetics.
HLA-B*58:01 carriers at high risk of allopurinol hypersensitivity (SJS/TEN).
HLA-B*15:02 carriers at high risk of carbamazepine-induced SJS/TEN.
HLA-B*57:01 carriers must not receive abacavir due to hypersensitivity reaction risk.
